candidates.observable_statement: ABB said it will invest $120 million to expand production of its low-voltage electrification products in the U.S. The investment will increase capacity in Tennessee and Mississippi to support growing demand from data centers, buildings and utilities.
candidates.capital_flow_implication: ABB will channel $120M into U.S. manufacturing, expanding capacity in Tennessee and Mississippi.
system_hints: U.S. manufacturing sector, low-voltage electrification products, data centers, buildings, utilities, Tennessee, Mississippi
candidates.physical_implication: Expansion of production capacity in Tennessee and Mississippi, likely involving construction of new facilities or expansion of existing ones.

candidates.observable_statement: Rolls-Royce will invest $75 million to expand its Aiken, South Carolina, engine plant to meet surging data center demand.
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Capital flow of $75M into the Aiken engine plant, expanding production capacity for large engines used in data centers and industrial facilities.
candidates.physical_implication: Expansion of the plant by 60,000 square feet, increasing production volume.

candidates.observable_statement: The 60,000-square-foot expansion will increase production volume for large engines used to power data centers and other industrial facilities.
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Physical expansion of the plant by 60,000 square feet, implying capital outflow for construction and equipment.
candidates.physical_implication: 60,000-square-foot expansion of the plant, increased production capacity.

candidates.observable_statement: Schneider Electric plans to invest $700 million in its U.S. operations through 2027 for facility upgrades, expansions and openings across eight sites.
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Capital outflow from Schneider Electric into U.S. manufacturing facilities, indicating increased investment in U.S. manufacturing capacity.
candidates.physical_implication: Facility upgrades, expansions, and openings across eight U.S. sites.

candidates.observable_statement: The move is driven by rising demand across data center, utilities, manufacturing and energy infrastructure segments.
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Capital inflow into U.S. data center, utilities, manufacturing, and energy infrastructure sectors.
candidates.physical_implication: Increased investment in U.S. manufacturing and data center facilities.
